Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A company needs to select a foundation model for a customer support chatbot. Which TWO factors are most important? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Model latency directly impacts user experience in real-time chat. Domain relevance ensures the model understands customer support language and patterns. Cost and accuracy for the specific task should be evaluated through benchmarking before committing to production.

Option2: Relevance to customer support domain
Option3: Total number of model parameters only

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is the primary purpose of an embedding model in a generative AI application?
Explanation: Embedding models convert text, images, or other data into dense numerical vectors that capture semantic meaning. These vectors enable similarity search, clustering, and retrieval operations. In RAG systems, embeddings allow finding documents semantically related to a user query even without exact keyword matches.
Option1: Converting data into numerical vectors that capture semantic meaning
Option2: Generating human-readable text responses
Option3: Training new foundation models from scratch
Option4: Compressing files for storage in S3

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which THREE considerations apply when integrating a foundation model into an existing enterprise application? (Select THREE)
Explanation: API rate limits and throttling affect application scalability and must be planned for. Data privacy requirements determine whether data can be sent to external model APIs or must stay on-premises. Latency requirements dictate whether synchronous real-time inference or asynchronous batch processing is appropriate for the use case.
Option1: API rate limits and throttling thresholds
Option2: Data privacy and residency requirements
Option3: Latency requirements for the use case
Option4: The color scheme of the application UI
Option5: The programming language the model was written in
Option6: The physical location of the model provider's office

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A company stores sensitive financial data and wants to use generative AI without data leaving their AWS account. Which approach is MOST appropriate?
Explanation: Amazon Bedrock processes inference requests within the customer's chosen AWS region. Customer data is not used to train models and does not leave the account. Combined with VPC endpoints, this ensures complete data residency while still accessing powerful foundation models.
Option1: Use Amazon Bedrock with VPC endpoints for private connectivity
Option2: Send data to a public third-party API
Option3: Host the model on a personal laptop
Option4: Use a free online chatbot service

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is model versioning and why is it important for production AI applications?
Explanation: Model versioning tracks different iterations of models used in production, including which version generated specific outputs. This enables rollback to previous versions if a new model degrades quality, supports A/B testing between versions, and provides audit trails for compliance showing which model version made each decision.
Option1: Tracking model iterations for rollback, A/B testing, and audit compliance
Option2: Storing multiple copies of the same model for backup
Option3: Automatically upgrading to the newest model always
Option4: Deleting old models to save storage space

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which TWO are key components of a data management strategy for RAG applications? (Select TWO)
Explanation: A document ingestion pipeline handles extracting, cleaning, chunking, and embedding new documents as they arrive. A metadata tagging strategy enables filtering search results by attributes like date, department, or document type, improving retrieval precision beyond pure semantic similarity.
Option1: Automated document ingestion and processing pipeline
Option2: Metadata tagging strategy for filtered retrieval
Option3: Storing all documents as raw binary blobs
Option4: Deleting documents after they are embedded

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A legal firm wants to ensure their AI system only references approved legal documents. How should the knowledge base be configured?
Explanation: Restricting the knowledge base data sources to only approved document repositories ensures the model can only retrieve and reference vetted legal content. Combined with source attribution in responses, lawyers can verify every citation traces back to an approved document, maintaining legal accuracy and compliance.
Option1: Restrict knowledge base sources to approved document repositories with source attribution
Option2: Allow the model to search the entire internet
Option3: Disable RAG and rely on the model's training data
Option4: Use the largest possible context window instead

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is the difference between a managed AI service and a self-hosted model deployment?
Explanation: Managed services like Bedrock handle infrastructure, scaling, patching, and availability automatically via API. Self-hosted deployments on EC2 or EKS give full control over the model, hardware, and configuration but require managing GPUs, scaling, updates, and availability yourself. The tradeoff is operational burden versus control and customization.
Option1: Managed services handle infrastructure automatically; self-hosted requires manual management
Option2: Managed services are always cheaper
Option3: Self-hosted models are always more accurate
Option4: There is no difference between them

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which TWO compliance requirements should be evaluated before deploying a foundation model? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Data residency regulations (like GDPR) may require that data stays within specific geographic regions during processing. Industry-specific regulations (like HIPAA for healthcare or SOX for finance) impose additional requirements on how AI systems handle, store, and process sensitive data, affecting model selection and architecture decisions.
Option1: Data residency and geographic processing requirements
Option2: Industry-specific regulations like HIPAA or SOX
Option3: The model provider's marketing materials
Option4: The number of languages the model supports

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A company needs to process both text documents and scanned images in their RAG system. What type of model is needed for the images?
Explanation: A multimodal embedding model or an OCR service like Amazon Textract is needed to extract text from scanned images before embedding. Textract converts scanned documents into machine-readable text that can then be chunked and embedded alongside native text documents in the same vector database.
Option1: OCR service like Textract to extract text from images before embedding
Option2: A text-only embedding model applied directly to images
Option3: A speech-to-text model

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is prompt caching and how does it reduce costs in production?
Explanation: Prompt caching stores the processed representation of frequently used system prompts or common prompt prefixes so they do not need to be reprocessed with every request. This reduces input token costs and latency for applications where the same system prompt or context is sent repeatedly across many user requests.
Option1: Storing processed prompt representations to avoid reprocessing common prefixes
Option2: Caching model weights on local disk
Option3: Storing user responses in a database

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which THREE are best practices for managing training data for fine-tuning? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Data quality validation ensures training examples are accurate and well-formatted before fine-tuning. Version controlling datasets enables reproducibility and rollback if a fine-tuned model underperforms. Removing PII from training data prevents the model from memorizing and potentially leaking sensitive personal information in its outputs.
Option1: Validate data quality before fine-tuning
Option2: Version control training datasets
Option3: Remove PII from training data
Option4: Use as much unvalidated data as possible
Option5: Never update training data once collected

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A startup wants to prototype a generative AI app quickly with minimal infrastructure. Which AWS service is best?
Explanation: Amazon Bedrock provides API access to multiple foundation models without provisioning any infrastructure. Developers can start building immediately by calling the API, test different models, and iterate on prompts without managing servers, GPUs, or model deployments, making it ideal for rapid prototyping.
Option1: Amazon Bedrock for serverless API access to foundation models
Option2: Self-hosting open-source models on EC2 GPU instances
Option3: Building a custom model from scratch on SageMaker
Option4: Using Amazon EMR for model training

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is the role of a model gateway or proxy in enterprise AI architectures?
Explanation: A model gateway sits between applications and foundation model APIs, providing centralized control over routing, rate limiting, cost tracking, logging, and failover. It enables switching between model providers without changing application code, enforces usage policies, and aggregates metrics across all AI applications in the organization.
Option1: Centralized routing, rate limiting, logging, and failover for model API calls
Option2: A physical device that connects to the internet
Option3: A tool for training new models
Option4: A database for storing model weights

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which TWO determine the optimal chunk size when preparing documents for a RAG knowledge base? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Document structure matters because technical manuals with short sections need smaller chunks than narrative documents with flowing paragraphs. Query patterns matter because if users ask specific factual questions, smaller chunks provide more precise retrieval, while broader analytical questions benefit from larger chunks that preserve more context.
Option1: The structure and format of the source documents
Option2: Typical user query patterns and specificity
Option3: The physical size of the S3 bucket
Option4: The number of AWS regions available

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is a model card and why should it be reviewed before selecting a foundation model?
Explanation: A model card documents the model's intended uses, known limitations, training data characteristics, evaluation benchmarks, and ethical considerations. Reviewing it before selection ensures the model is appropriate for your use case, reveals potential biases or weaknesses, and helps assess compliance with your organization's responsible AI policies.
Option1: Documentation of model capabilities, limitations, and ethical considerations for informed selection
Option2: A credit card used to pay for model API calls
Option3: A physical card shipped with hardware purchases
Option4: A configuration file for model deployment

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A company needs their RAG system to return different results based on user department. How should this be implemented?
Explanation: Metadata filtering in the vector database allows restricting search results based on attributes like department, access level, or document classification. When a user queries the system, their department metadata is included as a filter, ensuring they only receive results from documents they are authorized to access.
Option1: Metadata filtering on department attribute during vector search
Option2: Create separate knowledge bases per department
Option3: Fine-tune a different model per department
Option4: Disable RAG and use the base model only

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which TWO are advantages of using a model marketplace like Amazon Bedrock over a single model provider? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Access to multiple model providers through a single API lets you choose the best model for each task without vendor lock-in. Easy model switching enables rapid experimentation and fallback options if one provider has an outage or deprecates a model, improving application resilience.
Option1: Access to multiple providers without vendor lock-in
Option2: Easy switching between models for experimentation and resilience
Option3: All models are free on marketplaces

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is the difference between zero-shot, few-shot, and fine-tuned model performance?
Explanation: Zero-shot relies entirely on pre-trained knowledge with no examples, offering fastest setup but lowest task-specific accuracy. Few-shot provides examples in the prompt, improving accuracy without training. Fine-tuning retrains the model on task-specific data, achieving highest accuracy but requiring data preparation, compute cost, and ongoing maintenance.
Option1: Zero-shot is fastest to deploy; few-shot improves with examples; fine-tuning gives highest accuracy
Option2: All three produce identical results
Option3: Fine-tuning is always the cheapest option

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A healthcare company must ensure AI-generated clinical summaries are traceable to source records. Which capability is essential?
Explanation: Source attribution (citation) links each part of the AI-generated response back to the specific source documents used. In healthcare, this traceability is critical for clinical validation, regulatory compliance, and liability — clinicians must verify that every AI-generated statement is supported by actual patient records.
Option1: Source attribution linking responses to specific source documents
Option2: Increasing model temperature for creative responses
Option3: Using the largest available model

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which THREE factors affect the total cost of running a foundation model in production? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Input token count determines the cost of processing each request's prompt and context. Output token count determines the cost of generated responses. Request volume multiplied by per-token pricing determines total spend. Provisioned throughput adds a fixed cost but reduces per-request pricing for high-volume applications.
Option1: Input token count per request
Option2: Output token count per request
Option3: Total request volume
Option4: The color of the application's logo
Option5: The timezone of the development team
Option6: The number of IAM users in the account

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is a vector index and why is its choice important for RAG performance?
Explanation: A vector index is the data structure used to organize embeddings for fast similarity search. Different index types (HNSW, IVF, flat) trade off between search speed, accuracy, and memory usage. HNSW provides fast approximate search for large datasets, while flat indexes give exact results but are slower at scale.
Option1: Data structure organizing embeddings that trades off speed, accuracy, and memory
Option2: A SQL database index on a text column
Option3: A list of all model versions
Option4: A table of contents for PDF documents

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A company wants to use generative AI but their data contains trade secrets. Which TWO protections should they implement? (Select TWO)
Explanation: VPC endpoints ensure API traffic stays on the private AWS network, preventing data exposure over the public internet. Verifying the model provider's data usage policy confirms that inputs and outputs are not used for model training or shared with third parties, protecting intellectual property.
Option1: VPC endpoints for private API connectivity
Option2: Verify model provider does not use customer data for training
Option3: Post all data publicly for transparency
Option4: Use the cheapest model regardless of security

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is semantic chunking and how does it differ from fixed-size chunking?
Explanation: Fixed-size chunking splits documents at arbitrary character or token boundaries, potentially breaking sentences or ideas mid-thought. Semantic chunking uses NLP to split at natural boundaries like paragraph breaks, topic changes, or sentence endings, preserving meaning within each chunk and improving retrieval quality.
Option1: Splitting at natural meaning boundaries versus arbitrary fixed character counts
Option2: Compressing chunks to save storage
Option3: Encrypting chunks before storage

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which TWO metrics should be tracked to evaluate a RAG system's retrieval quality? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Retrieval precision measures what fraction of retrieved documents are actually relevant to the query. Retrieval recall measures what fraction of all relevant documents in the knowledge base were successfully retrieved. Together they indicate whether the system finds the right documents without returning too much noise.
Option1: Retrieval precision (fraction of retrieved docs that are relevant)
Option2: Retrieval recall (fraction of relevant docs that are retrieved)
Option3: Total S3 storage used
Option4: Number of IAM policies in the account

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A company fine-tunes a model on Bedrock. Can they use the fine-tuned model outside of Bedrock?
Explanation: No. Models fine-tuned on Bedrock are accessible only through the Bedrock API within your AWS account. The fine-tuned model weights cannot be downloaded or exported. This is a key consideration when evaluating vendor lock-in — if portability is required, fine-tuning open-source models on SageMaker may be more appropriate.
Option1: No, fine-tuned Bedrock models are only accessible through the Bedrock API
Option2: Yes, you can download and run them anywhere
Option3: Yes, they are automatically published publicly
Option4: Yes, AWS ships the weights on a Snowball device

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: What is hybrid search and why is it used in RAG systems?
Explanation: Hybrid search combines semantic vector search with traditional keyword search. Vector search excels at finding conceptually similar content but may miss exact terms like product codes or names. Keyword search catches exact matches. Combining both with score fusion provides more comprehensive and accurate retrieval than either alone.
Option1: Combining vector semantic search with keyword search for better retrieval accuracy
Option2: Searching across two different AWS regions simultaneously
Option3: Using two different embedding models at once
Option4: Running searches on both CPU and GPU

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: Which TWO are signs that a foundation model needs to be replaced or upgraded in production? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Declining output quality detected through automated evaluation metrics or increased user complaints indicates the model may no longer meet requirements. A newer model offering significantly better performance at lower cost represents an opportunity to improve the application while reducing spend.
Option1: Declining output quality detected through evaluation metrics
Option2: A newer model offers better performance at lower cost
Option3: The model has been running for exactly 30 days
Option4: The AWS console UI was updated

QuestionText: A development team is building a chatbot using Bedrock. Which TWO are essential components of the application architecture? (Select TWO)
Explanation: A conversation history manager maintains context across multi-turn conversations by storing and sending previous messages with each request. An error handling layer manages API failures, rate limiting, and timeout scenarios gracefully, ensuring the application degrades gracefully rather than crashing when issues occur.
Option1: Conversation history manager for multi-turn context
Option2: Error handling for API failures and rate limits
Option3: A physical server room

QuestionText: What is the purpose of an orchestration layer in a generative AI application?
Explanation: The orchestration layer coordinates the flow between user input, retrieval systems, model inference, guardrails, and response delivery. It manages the sequence of operations — for example, first retrieving context from a knowledge base, then constructing the prompt, calling the model, checking guardrails, and returning the filtered response to the user.
Option1: Coordinating the flow between retrieval, inference, guardrails, and response delivery
Option2: Managing the physical network infrastructure
Option3: Training foundation models from scratch

QuestionText: Which THREE should be included in an API integration with Amazon Bedrock? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Retry logic with exponential backoff handles transient API failures and throttling gracefully. Request timeout configuration prevents the application from hanging indefinitely on slow responses. Response streaming processes tokens as they arrive rather than waiting for the complete response, improving perceived latency for users.
Option1: Retry logic with exponential backoff
Option2: Request timeout configuration
Option3: Response streaming for incremental output
Option4: Hardcoded API keys in source code

QuestionText: A company wants to add generative AI summarization to their existing Java application. What is the simplest integration approach?
Explanation: The AWS SDK for Java provides native Bedrock API clients that integrate directly into existing Java applications. Developers call the InvokeModel API with their prompt and receive the response, requiring minimal code changes to add AI capabilities to an existing application.
Option1: Use the AWS SDK for Java to call the Bedrock InvokeModel API
Option2: Rewrite the entire application in Python
Option3: Deploy a separate microservice on EC2 with GPUs
Option4: Use a browser-based chatbot widget only

QuestionText: What is the purpose of a prompt template in production applications?
Explanation: Prompt templates separate the static prompt structure (instructions, format, persona) from dynamic variables (user input, retrieved context, metadata). This enables consistent prompt construction across requests, easier maintenance and versioning, and prevents prompt injection by clearly delineating system instructions from user-provided content.
Option1: Separating static prompt structure from dynamic variables for consistency and security
Option2: A visual design template for the chat UI
Option3: A pre-written response the model always returns
Option4: A template for creating AWS CloudFormation stacks

QuestionText: Which TWO are best practices for managing conversation history in a multi-turn chatbot? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Limiting history to the most recent N turns prevents exceeding the context window and controls cost, since all history tokens count as input. Summarizing older conversation turns preserves important context while reducing token count, allowing longer conversations without losing critical earlier information.
Option1: Limit history to the most recent N turns
Option2: Summarize older turns to preserve context while reducing tokens
Option3: Send the entire conversation history every time regardless of length
Option4: Delete all history after each response

QuestionText: A company needs to process 50000 documents through an LLM for classification overnight. Which inference pattern is best?
Explanation: Batch inference processes large volumes of requests asynchronously without maintaining a real-time endpoint. On Bedrock, batch inference reads prompts from S3 and writes results back to S3, optimizing throughput and cost for high-volume offline processing where immediate responses are not required.
Option1: Batch inference processing from S3 input to S3 output
Option2: Real-time synchronous API calls in a loop
Option3: Manual copy-paste into a chat interface

QuestionText: What is function calling in the context of LLM applications?
Explanation: Function calling allows an LLM to generate structured JSON that specifies which function to call and with what parameters, rather than generating free-form text. The application then executes the function and optionally feeds the result back to the model. This enables LLMs to interact with databases, APIs, and tools reliably.
Option1: LLM generates structured function calls that the application executes
Option2: The LLM directly executes code on the server
Option3: A method for training models faster
Option4: Calling AWS Lambda functions from the console

QuestionText: Which THREE are components of a production-ready RAG implementation? (Select THREE)
Explanation: A document processing pipeline handles ingestion, chunking, and embedding of source documents. A vector database stores embeddings and serves similarity queries. A response generation layer combines retrieved context with the user query in a prompt and calls the foundation model to produce the final answer.
Option1: Document processing pipeline for ingestion and embedding
Option2: Vector database for similarity search
Option3: Response generation layer combining context with the query
Option4: A physical filing cabinet for document storage
Option6: A fax machine for document input

QuestionText: A microservices architecture needs multiple services to call Bedrock. How should authentication be managed?
Explanation: Each microservice should use its own IAM role with least-privilege permissions scoped to only the Bedrock actions and models it needs. IAM roles provide temporary credentials that rotate automatically, eliminating the security risk of shared or hardcoded access keys across services.
Option1: Individual IAM roles per service with least-privilege Bedrock permissions
Option2: Share one set of access keys across all services
Option3: Hardcode credentials in each service's config file
Option4: Use the root account for all API calls

QuestionText: What is the purpose of output parsing in a generative AI application?
Explanation: Output parsing extracts structured data from the model's free-form text response. Since LLMs generate natural language, parsing is needed to extract specific fields, validate format compliance (like JSON), handle edge cases where the model deviates from the expected format, and convert the output into data structures the application can use.
Option1: Extracting structured data from free-form model responses for application use
Option2: Compressing the response to save bandwidth
Option3: Translating the response to another language

QuestionText: Which TWO caching strategies reduce cost and latency in generative AI applications? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Semantic caching stores responses for semantically similar queries (not just exact matches) so repeated similar questions return cached answers instantly. Prompt prefix caching stores the processed representation of common system prompts to avoid reprocessing the same instructions with every request, reducing input token costs.
Option1: Semantic caching for similar queries
Option2: Prompt prefix caching for repeated system prompts
Option3: Caching model weights on the client device
Option4: Caching IAM policies in the browser

QuestionText: A company wants to add AI-powered search to their existing website. Which integration approach requires the least code changes?
Explanation: Amazon Kendra provides a drop-in search widget and API that can be embedded into existing websites with minimal code. It handles document indexing, natural language understanding, and returns precise answers with source links, requiring only API calls or a JavaScript widget to integrate.
Option1: Amazon Kendra search API or embeddable widget
Option2: Building a custom search engine from scratch
Option3: Replacing the entire website with a chatbot
Option4: Training a custom model on SageMaker

QuestionText: What is the circuit breaker pattern and why is it important for AI API integrations?
Explanation: The circuit breaker pattern stops sending requests to a failing API after a threshold of failures, returning a fallback response instead. This prevents cascading failures when the AI service is down, protects the application from hanging on timeouts, and allows the system to recover gracefully when the service returns.
Option1: Stopping requests to a failing API and returning fallback responses to prevent cascading failures
Option2: A physical electrical safety device
Option3: A method for training models faster
Option4: A technique for compressing API responses

QuestionText: Which TWO are important when implementing logging for generative AI applications? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Logging prompts and responses enables debugging, quality monitoring, and compliance auditing of AI interactions. Logging latency and token usage per request enables cost tracking, performance monitoring, and capacity planning. Both should be implemented with appropriate PII redaction to protect user privacy.
Option1: Log prompts and responses for debugging and compliance
Option2: Log latency and token usage for cost and performance monitoring
Option3: Log user passwords in plain text
Option4: Disable all logging to reduce costs

QuestionText: A company needs their AI application to fall back to a simpler model if the primary model is unavailable. What pattern should they implement?
Explanation: A model fallback chain defines a priority list of models. If the primary model times out or returns an error, the application automatically retries with the next model in the chain. This provides resilience against single-model outages and can also route simpler queries to cheaper models for cost optimization.
Option1: Model fallback chain with automatic retry on secondary models
Option2: Only use one model and show an error if it fails
Option3: Queue all requests until the primary model recovers
Option4: Ask users to try again later

QuestionText: Which THREE are best practices for deploying generative AI applications to production? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Gradual rollout (canary deployment) routes a small percentage of traffic to the new version first, catching issues before full deployment. Automated rollback triggers revert to the previous version if error rates spike. Health checks continuously verify the application and model endpoints are responding correctly.
Option1: Gradual canary rollout with percentage-based traffic shifting
Option2: Automated rollback on error rate thresholds
Option3: Continuous health checks on model endpoints
Option4: Deploy directly to 100% of traffic immediately

QuestionText: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous API patterns for AI applications?
Explanation: Synchronous patterns block the caller until the response is complete, suitable for real-time chat. Asynchronous patterns submit the request and poll or receive a callback when complete, suitable for long-running tasks like document processing. Async patterns prevent timeout issues and allow better resource utilization for batch workloads.
Option1: Synchronous blocks until complete for real-time; async submits and polls for batch workloads
Option2: Synchronous is always faster
Option3: Asynchronous requires a different programming language

QuestionText: What is a jailbreak attack on an LLM and how can it be mitigated?
Explanation: A jailbreak attack uses crafted prompts to bypass the model's safety training and content policies, tricking it into generating prohibited content. Mitigations include Bedrock Guardrails for input/output filtering, robust system prompts with explicit safety rules, and input validation that detects common jailbreak patterns before they reach the model.
Option1: Crafted prompts bypassing safety training — mitigated with Guardrails and input validation
Option2: A physical security breach of a data center
Option3: A method for improving model accuracy
Option4: A technique for reducing inference latency

QuestionText: Which TWO Bedrock Guardrails capabilities protect against harmful content? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Content filters evaluate inputs and outputs against configurable thresholds for harmful categories including hate, insults, sexual content, violence, and misconduct. Word filters block specific words or phrases (like competitor names or profanity) from appearing in model inputs or outputs using exact match or pattern matching.
Option1: Content filters for hate, violence, sexual, and misconduct categories
Option2: Word and phrase filters for blocked terms
Option3: Automatic model retraining

QuestionText: A company must comply with GDPR when using generative AI. Which THREE requirements apply? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Users have the right to know when AI is making decisions about them (transparency). Personal data used in prompts must be minimized and processed lawfully (data minimization). Users can request deletion of their personal data from AI systems (right to erasure). All three are core GDPR principles affecting AI deployments.
Option1: Transparency about AI-driven decisions
Option2: Data minimization in prompts and processing
Option3: Right to erasure of personal data
Option6: Sharing data with all model providers

QuestionText: What is the principle of least privilege as applied to Bedrock API access?
Explanation: Each application or user should have IAM permissions granting only the specific Bedrock actions (like InvokeModel) and model IDs they need. A customer support bot should not have access to image generation models. This limits the blast radius if credentials are compromised and prevents unauthorized model usage.
Option1: Grant only specific Bedrock actions and model IDs needed for each application
Option2: Give all users full Bedrock administrator access
Option3: Use the root account for all Bedrock calls
Option4: Share one API key across all applications

QuestionText: Which TWO protect sensitive data in Bedrock API requests? (Select TWO)
Explanation: VPC endpoints route Bedrock API traffic through the private AWS network using PrivateLink, preventing data from traversing the public internet. TLS encryption ensures all data in transit between the application and Bedrock is encrypted, protecting against interception even if traffic is routed over public networks.
Option1: VPC endpoints for private network connectivity
Option2: TLS encryption for all data in transit
Option3: Sending data over unencrypted HTTP
Option4: Posting prompts to a public forum for review

QuestionText: What is an AI governance framework and why do organizations need one?
Explanation: An AI governance framework defines the policies, processes, roles, and controls for developing and deploying AI responsibly. It establishes who approves AI use cases, how models are evaluated for bias and safety, how incidents are handled, and how compliance is maintained. Without governance, AI deployments risk legal, ethical, and reputational harm.
Option1: Policies and processes defining responsible AI development, deployment, and oversight
Option2: A software library for building AI models
Option3: An AWS service for model training
Option4: A type of neural network architecture

QuestionText: A model generates a response containing a customer's credit card number from the prompt context. Which Guardrails feature prevents this?
Explanation: Guardrails sensitive information filters detect and redact PII patterns like credit card numbers, SSNs, email addresses, and phone numbers from both model inputs and outputs. This prevents the model from echoing back or exposing sensitive data that appears in the prompt context or retrieved documents.
Option1: Guardrails sensitive information (PII) filters
Option2: Guardrails content filters
Option3: Guardrails denied topics

QuestionText: Which THREE are components of an AI incident response plan? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Defined escalation procedures ensure the right people are notified when AI systems produce harmful outputs. A model rollback process enables quick reversion to a safe previous version. Post-incident review analyzes root causes and implements preventive measures. Together these components enable rapid, organized response to AI safety incidents.
Option1: Defined escalation procedures for harmful outputs
Option2: Model rollback process to a safe previous version
Option3: Post-incident review and root cause analysis
Option5: Deleting all logs to hide the incident

QuestionText: What is contextual grounding in Bedrock Guardrails?
Explanation: Contextual grounding checks verify that the model's response is factually supported by the source documents provided in the prompt context. If the model generates claims not found in the retrieved documents (hallucinations), the grounding check flags or blocks the response, ensuring outputs are anchored in verified information.
Option1: Verifying model responses are supported by provided source documents to prevent hallucination
Option2: Connecting the model to a physical ground wire
Option3: Training the model on more data

QuestionText: Which TWO are essential for auditing AI system usage in an enterprise? (Select TWO)
Explanation: CloudTrail logging of all Bedrock API calls provides a complete audit trail of who called which model, when, and from where. Application-level logging of prompts and responses (with PII redacted) enables quality review, compliance verification, and investigation of specific AI interactions when issues are reported.
Option1: CloudTrail logging of all Bedrock API calls
Option2: Application-level logging of prompts and responses with PII redaction
Option3: Disabling all logging to save costs
Option4: Only logging errors and ignoring successful calls

QuestionText: A financial services company wants to use AI for loan decisions. What regulatory considerations apply?
Explanation: Financial AI decisions must be explainable to applicants (fair lending laws require adverse action notices explaining why). Models must be tested for disparate impact across protected classes. Decisions must be auditable with complete records. Human oversight must be maintained for consequential financial decisions affecting consumers.
Option1: Explainability, bias testing, auditability, and human oversight for financial decisions
Option2: No regulations apply to AI in finance
Option3: Only the model's accuracy matters
Option4: Automated decisions require no human review

QuestionText: What is the difference between content filtering and topic blocking in Guardrails?
Explanation: Content filters evaluate text against harmful categories (hate, violence, sexual) using severity thresholds — they assess the nature of the content. Topic blocking uses natural language descriptions to define entire subjects the model must refuse to discuss (like investment advice or competitor products) — it restricts the scope of conversation.
Option1: Content filters assess harmfulness by category; topic blocking restricts entire conversation subjects
Option2: They are identical features with different names
Option3: Content filtering only works on images
Option4: Topic blocking only works in specific AWS regions

QuestionText: Which TWO help prevent prompt injection attacks? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Input validation and sanitization detect and neutralize common injection patterns before they reach the model. Separating system instructions from user input using structured prompt templates makes it harder for user content to override system-level instructions, as the model can distinguish between trusted and untrusted content.
Option1: Input validation and sanitization of user content
Option2: Structured prompt templates separating system and user content
Option3: Increasing model temperature

QuestionText: A company deploys an AI chatbot that accidentally provides medical advice. What governance control should have prevented this?
Explanation: A use case approval process within the AI governance framework should evaluate each AI application for regulatory risk before deployment. Medical advice requires licensed professionals and regulatory compliance. The governance review would have identified this risk and either blocked the use case or required appropriate safeguards and disclaimers.
Option1: Use case approval process evaluating regulatory risk before deployment
Option2: Deploying faster without any review process
Option3: Using a larger model to improve accuracy
Option4: Adding more training data about medicine

QuestionText: Which THREE are pillars of responsible AI that should be evaluated before deploying any AI system? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Fairness ensures the system does not discriminate against protected groups. Transparency ensures users understand they are interacting with AI and how decisions are made. Safety ensures the system does not produce harmful outputs or take dangerous actions. All three must be assessed and addressed before production deployment.
Option1: Fairness across demographic groups
Option2: Transparency about AI usage and decision-making
Option3: Safety against harmful outputs and actions

QuestionText: What is model abuse and how can it be detected?
Explanation: Model abuse occurs when users intentionally misuse an AI system for harmful purposes like generating misinformation, phishing emails, or malicious code. Detection involves monitoring usage patterns for anomalies, flagging high volumes of Guardrails violations from specific users, and analyzing blocked content categories to identify systematic abuse attempts.
Option1: Intentional misuse detected through usage monitoring and Guardrails violation patterns
Option2: A model that produces slow responses
Option3: Using a model for its intended purpose
Option4: A model that costs too much to run

QuestionText: Which TWO ensure data privacy when fine-tuning a model on Bedrock? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Training data encryption with KMS ensures fine-tuning data is protected at rest with customer-controlled keys. The fine-tuned model being private to your account means neither AWS nor the model provider can access your custom model or the data used to create it, maintaining complete data confidentiality.
Option1: Training data encryption with customer-managed KMS keys
Option2: Fine-tuned model is private and inaccessible to AWS or model provider
Option3: Training data is shared with the model provider

QuestionText: A company wants to track which team members are using AI and how much they spend. Which AWS feature enables this?
Explanation: AWS Cost Allocation Tags applied to Bedrock resources and API calls enable tracking AI usage and costs by team, project, or application. Combined with AWS Budgets alerts, organizations can monitor spending per team and receive notifications when thresholds are approached or exceeded.
Option1: Cost allocation tags on Bedrock resources with AWS Budgets alerts
Option2: Checking the monthly AWS bill total only
Option3: Asking team members to self-report usage
Option4: Disabling cost tracking to reduce overhead

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A Bedrock application has unpredictable traffic with long idle periods. Which pricing model minimizes cost?
Explanation: On-demand pricing charges only for tokens processed per request with no minimum commitment. During idle periods there is zero cost. This is ideal for unpredictable or low-volume workloads where provisioned throughput would waste money on reserved capacity that sits unused.
Option1: On-demand pricing with pay-per-token
Option2: Provisioned Throughput with reserved capacity
Option3: Running a self-hosted model on dedicated EC2 instances

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: Which TWO reduce inference costs for high-volume Bedrock applications? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Prompt optimization reduces input tokens by removing unnecessary instructions, verbose context, or redundant examples from prompts. Response length limits via max_tokens prevent the model from generating unnecessarily long responses. Both directly reduce the total tokens processed per request, lowering per-request cost.
Option1: Optimize prompts to reduce input token count
Option2: Set max_tokens to limit response length
Option3: Use the largest model for every request

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: What is model routing and how does it optimize cost?
Explanation: Model routing directs requests to different models based on task complexity. Simple queries (like greetings or FAQs) route to smaller, cheaper models while complex queries (like multi-step reasoning) route to larger, more capable models. This avoids paying premium model prices for tasks that simpler models handle equally well.
Option1: Directing requests to appropriately sized models based on task complexity
Option2: Sending all requests to the most expensive model
Option3: Randomly selecting a model for each request
Option4: Using only one model for all tasks

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: Which THREE are key metrics for monitoring a generative AI application in production? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Latency (time to first token and total response time) measures user experience. Error rate tracks API failures and timeouts. Token usage per request enables cost monitoring and optimization. Together these metrics provide visibility into performance, reliability, and cost efficiency of the AI application.
Option1: Latency (time to first token and total response time)
Option2: Error rate and API failure count
Option3: Token usage per request
Option4: Number of developers on the team
Option5: Lines of code in the application

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A company notices their Bedrock costs are higher than expected. Which TWO are the most likely causes? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Excessively long system prompts sent with every request multiply input token costs across all requests. Unnecessarily large context windows stuffed with irrelevant retrieved documents increase input tokens without improving response quality. Both waste tokens on content that does not contribute to better answers.
Option1: Excessively long system prompts repeated with every request
Option2: Too much irrelevant context in RAG retrieval
Option3: Using VPC endpoints for security

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: What is the benefit of streaming responses in a user-facing AI application?
Explanation: Streaming returns tokens incrementally as they are generated rather than waiting for the complete response. Users see text appearing within milliseconds instead of waiting several seconds for the full response. This dramatically improves perceived latency and user experience even though total generation time is the same.
Option1: Improved perceived latency by showing tokens as they are generated
Option2: Reduced total model inference time
Option3: Lower token costs per request

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: Which TWO are benefits of using Provisioned Throughput on Bedrock? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Guaranteed capacity ensures your application always has dedicated model inference resources regardless of other customers' usage. Consistent low latency eliminates the variability of on-demand pricing where response times can fluctuate based on service load. Both are critical for production applications with strict SLAs.
Option1: Guaranteed dedicated inference capacity
Option2: Consistent low-latency responses
Option3: Lower cost for sporadic low-volume usage

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A RAG application retrieves too many irrelevant documents, increasing cost and reducing quality. How should this be fixed?
Explanation: Tuning the retrieval parameters — reducing the number of retrieved chunks (top-K), increasing the similarity threshold to filter low-relevance results, and adding metadata filters to narrow the search scope — improves precision. Better chunking strategies and embedding model selection also improve retrieval relevance.
Option1: Reduce top-K, increase similarity threshold, and add metadata filters
Option2: Retrieve all documents in the knowledge base every time
Option3: Disable RAG and use the base model only

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: What is the purpose of A/B testing different prompts in production?
Explanation: A/B testing routes a percentage of traffic to different prompt versions and measures which produces better outcomes (higher user satisfaction, fewer escalations, better accuracy). This data-driven approach replaces guesswork with evidence, enabling continuous prompt optimization based on real user interactions and measurable business metrics.
Option1: Measuring which prompt version produces better outcomes with real user traffic
Option2: Testing if the API endpoint is reachable
Option3: Comparing two different AWS regions

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: Which THREE are operational best practices for production AI applications? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Automated alerting on error rates and latency spikes enables rapid incident response before users are significantly impacted. Regular prompt review and optimization prevents cost drift and quality degradation over time. Capacity planning based on usage trends ensures the application can handle growth without performance degradation.
Option1: Automated alerting on error rates and latency
Option2: Regular prompt review and optimization
Option3: Capacity planning based on usage trends
Option4: Manual monitoring by checking logs once a month

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A company runs the same summarization prompt on thousands of similar documents daily. How can they reduce redundant processing?
Explanation: Semantic caching identifies when a new request is semantically similar to a previously processed one and returns the cached response. For document summarization where many documents share similar content, this can dramatically reduce the number of actual model invocations needed, cutting costs and latency.
Option1: Implement semantic caching to reuse responses for similar inputs
Option2: Process each document individually every time
Option3: Use a larger model for faster processing
Option4: Disable all caching for freshest results

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: Which TWO help right-size model selection for different use cases? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Benchmarking multiple models on your specific task with representative data reveals which models meet quality requirements at what cost. Analyzing the complexity distribution of incoming requests identifies what percentage can be handled by smaller cheaper models versus requiring larger more capable ones.
Option1: Benchmark models on your specific task with representative data
Option2: Analyze request complexity to route appropriately
Option3: Always use the newest model available
Option4: Select based on model name recognition only

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: What is token budgeting and why is it important for cost management?
Explanation: Token budgeting sets maximum input and output token limits per request, per user, or per application. This prevents runaway costs from unexpectedly long prompts or responses, enables predictable billing, and forces prompt optimization. Combined with monitoring and alerts, it provides comprehensive cost control for AI applications.
Option1: Setting token limits per request or user to control costs and enable predictable billing
Option2: Allocating physical tokens like arcade coins
Option3: A method for training models with less data
Option4: A technique for compressing model weights

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A company wants to reduce latency for their Bedrock application. Which TWO approaches help? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Using a smaller model that still meets quality requirements reduces inference time since smaller models generate tokens faster. Reducing input prompt length by trimming unnecessary context decreases the time the model spends processing the input before generating the first output token.
Option1: Use a smaller model that meets quality requirements
Option2: Reduce input prompt length by trimming unnecessary context
Option3: Add more irrelevant context to the prompt
Option4: Switch to batch inference for real-time requests

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: What is the purpose of usage quotas and rate limiting in AI applications?
Explanation: Usage quotas cap the total number of requests or tokens a user or application can consume within a time period, preventing abuse and controlling costs. Rate limiting caps requests per second, protecting the backend from traffic spikes and ensuring fair resource sharing across users and applications.
Option1: Preventing abuse, controlling costs, and ensuring fair resource sharing
Option2: Slowing down the application intentionally
Option3: Reducing model accuracy to save money
Option4: Blocking all users from accessing the application

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: Which THREE should be included in a cost optimization review for AI applications? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Prompt efficiency analysis identifies opportunities to reduce token count without sacrificing quality. Model selection review ensures each use case uses the most cost-effective model that meets requirements. Caching effectiveness measurement quantifies how much redundant processing is being avoided and identifies opportunities for improvement.
Option1: Prompt efficiency analysis for token reduction
Option2: Model selection review for cost-effectiveness
Option3: Caching effectiveness measurement
Option4: Increasing all model sizes for better quality
Option5: Removing all monitoring to reduce overhead
Option6: Using on-demand for all workloads regardless of pattern

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A chatbot application experiences slow responses during peak hours. What is the most likely cause and solution?
Explanation: On-demand Bedrock inference can experience higher latency during peak demand periods when service capacity is shared. Provisioned Throughput dedicates capacity to your application, providing consistent performance regardless of overall service load. This is the recommended solution for applications with strict latency requirements.
Option1: Shared on-demand capacity — solve with Provisioned Throughput for dedicated capacity
Option2: The model needs retraining
Option3: The application needs more S3 storage

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: Which TWO indicate a RAG system needs optimization? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Users frequently reporting that the AI gives wrong answers despite relevant documents existing in the knowledge base indicates poor retrieval quality. High per-request costs with large numbers of retrieved chunks that do not improve answer quality indicate over-retrieval that wastes tokens and money.
Option1: Users report wrong answers despite relevant documents existing
Option2: High costs from retrieving too many irrelevant chunks
Option3: The application responds in under one second
Option4: Users are satisfied with answer quality

QuestionText: What is the purpose of evaluation datasets when testing a generative AI application?
Explanation: Evaluation datasets contain curated input-output pairs representing expected behavior across diverse scenarios. They enable automated testing of model responses against known-good answers using metrics like accuracy, ROUGE, or human ratings. This provides objective measurement of quality and catches regressions when prompts or models change.
Option1: Curated test cases enabling automated quality measurement against expected outputs
Option2: Datasets used to train the model
Option3: Random data generated for load testing
Option4: Production user data used without consent

QuestionText: Which TWO are essential types of tests for a RAG application? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Retrieval quality tests verify that the correct documents are returned for representative queries by measuring precision and recall against labeled relevance judgments. End-to-end response quality tests evaluate the final generated answer for accuracy, completeness, and faithfulness to source documents using automated metrics and human review.
Option1: Retrieval quality tests measuring precision and recall
Option2: End-to-end response quality tests for accuracy and faithfulness
Option3: CSS styling tests for the chat widget

QuestionText: A generative AI application produces inconsistent response formats. What is the most likely cause and fix?
Explanation: Inconsistent formatting usually results from vague or ambiguous prompt instructions. The fix is to add explicit format specifications in the system prompt (e.g., 'Always respond in JSON with fields: answer, confidence, sources'), provide few-shot examples of the desired format, and implement output parsing validation.
Option1: Vague prompt instructions — fix with explicit format specs and few-shot examples
Option2: The model needs more GPU memory
Option3: The VPC configuration is incorrect
Option4: The S3 bucket permissions are wrong

QuestionText: Which THREE should be included in a comprehensive AI testing strategy? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Unit tests validate individual components like prompt templates, parsing logic, and API integration. Integration tests verify the full pipeline from user input through retrieval, inference, and response delivery. Adversarial tests attempt prompt injection, jailbreaks, and edge cases to verify safety guardrails work correctly.
Option1: Unit tests for individual components
Option2: Integration tests for the full pipeline
Option3: Adversarial tests for safety and security
Option4: Tests that only check if the server starts
Option5: Tests that only verify CSS styling
Option6: Tests that only check database connectivity

QuestionText: A RAG application returns correct documents but the final answer is wrong. Where is the problem?
Explanation: If retrieval is correct but the answer is wrong, the issue is in the generation step. Common causes include: the prompt does not effectively instruct the model to use the retrieved context, the context is too long causing the model to miss key information, or the model hallucinates despite having correct context.
Option1: Generation step — prompt may not effectively direct the model to use retrieved context
Option2: The vector database is misconfigured
Option3: The embedding model is wrong
Option4: The S3 bucket is in the wrong region

QuestionText: Which TWO metrics evaluate the faithfulness of a RAG response to its source documents? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Groundedness measures whether claims in the response are supported by the retrieved documents. Answer relevancy measures whether the response actually addresses the user's question. Together they ensure the model both uses its sources correctly and stays on topic, catching both hallucination and irrelevant responses.
Option1: Groundedness (claims supported by source documents)
Option2: Answer relevancy (response addresses the question)
Option3: Total token count

QuestionText: A model suddenly starts producing lower quality responses after no code changes. What should be investigated first?
Explanation: Model provider updates can change model behavior without application code changes. Check if the model version was updated by the provider. Also check if the knowledge base documents changed, if data quality degraded in the retrieval pipeline, or if traffic patterns shifted to queries the model handles poorly.
Option1: Check if the model provider updated the model version
Option2: Restart all EC2 instances
Option3: Delete and recreate the S3 bucket

QuestionText: What is red teaming for AI applications and when should it be performed?
Explanation: Red teaming involves skilled testers deliberately trying to make the AI system fail through prompt injection, jailbreaks, adversarial inputs, and edge cases. It should be performed before production launch and periodically after deployment. Findings are used to strengthen guardrails, improve prompts, and patch vulnerabilities.
Option1: Deliberate adversarial testing before launch and periodically in production
Option2: A team that only tests the application's color scheme
Option3: Automated unit testing of API endpoints

QuestionText: Which TWO help diagnose why a Bedrock API call is failing? (Select TWO)
Explanation: CloudTrail logs capture every Bedrock API call with request parameters, response codes, and error messages, showing exactly what was sent and what failed. CloudWatch metrics for Bedrock show invocation counts, error rates, and latency patterns that reveal whether failures are sporadic or systematic.
Option1: CloudTrail logs showing API call details and error messages
Option2: CloudWatch metrics showing error rates and patterns
Option3: Checking the application's CSS files
Option4: Reviewing the S3 bucket's lifecycle policies

QuestionText: A chatbot passes all automated tests but users report poor quality. What type of evaluation is missing?
Explanation: Human evaluation captures subjective quality dimensions that automated metrics miss — like helpfulness, naturalness, tone appropriateness, and whether the response actually solves the user's problem. Automated metrics like ROUGE measure text overlap but cannot assess whether a response is truly useful to a human.
Option1: Human evaluation for subjective quality dimensions automated metrics cannot capture
Option2: More automated unit tests
Option3: Load testing with higher traffic

QuestionText: Which THREE are common causes of hallucination in RAG applications? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Insufficient or irrelevant retrieved context forces the model to fill gaps from its training data, which may be incorrect. Prompts that do not explicitly instruct the model to only use provided context allow it to generate from memory. Overly high temperature increases randomness, making the model more likely to generate unsupported claims.
Option1: Insufficient or irrelevant retrieved context
Option2: Prompts not constraining the model to use provided context only
Option3: Temperature set too high increasing randomness

QuestionText: A company wants to continuously monitor their AI application's response quality in production. Which approach is most effective?
Explanation: Automated evaluation pipelines sample production requests periodically, run them through quality scoring models (like LLM-as-judge), and track scores over time on dashboards with alerting. This catches quality degradation early without requiring manual review of every response, scaling quality monitoring to production volumes.
Option1: Automated evaluation pipeline sampling production requests with quality scoring and alerting
Option2: Manually reading every response
Option3: Only checking quality during initial deployment

QuestionText: Which TWO are effective strategies for testing prompt changes before production deployment? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Running the new prompt against a curated evaluation dataset with known-good answers catches regressions objectively. Shadow testing runs the new prompt alongside the current one on live traffic without serving the new responses to users, comparing quality metrics between versions in real conditions.
Option1: Evaluation dataset testing against known-good answers
Option2: Shadow testing alongside current prompt on live traffic
Option3: Deploying directly to all users and monitoring complaints
Option4: Testing with a single example input only

QuestionText: A Bedrock application returns ThrottlingException errors during peak hours. What are TWO solutions? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Implementing retry logic with exponential backoff handles transient throttling by spacing out retries. Requesting a service quota increase from AWS raises the requests-per-minute limit for your account, providing more headroom during peak periods. For sustained high traffic, Provisioned Throughput eliminates throttling entirely.
Option1: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff
Option2: Request a Bedrock service quota increase
Option3: Reduce the model's temperature setting
Option4: Delete CloudTrail logs to free resources

QuestionText: What is LLM-as-judge evaluation and when is it appropriate?
Explanation: LLM-as-judge uses a foundation model to evaluate the quality of another model's responses against criteria like accuracy, helpfulness, and coherence. It scales better than human evaluation for large volumes. It is appropriate for subjective quality assessment but should be validated against human judgments to ensure the judge model's ratings correlate with human preferences.
Option1: Using an LLM to evaluate another model's response quality at scale
Option2: A legal proceeding involving AI
Option3: A model that only generates legal documents
Option4: An AI system that replaces human judges

QuestionText: Which THREE should be tested during adversarial testing of an AI chatbot? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Prompt injection attempts test whether users can override system instructions. Attempts to extract the system prompt test information leakage. Requests for harmful content test whether content filters and guardrails effectively block prohibited outputs. All three represent real attack vectors that malicious users will attempt.
Option1: Prompt injection to override system instructions
Option2: System prompt extraction attempts
Option3: Requests for harmful or prohibited content
Option4: Whether the chatbot responds in under one second
Option5: Whether the UI renders correctly on mobile
Option6: Whether the database handles concurrent writes

QuestionText: A RAG application retrieves the right documents but the model ignores them and answers from its own knowledge. How is this fixed?
Explanation: The prompt must explicitly instruct the model to base its answer only on the provided context and to say it does not know if the context does not contain the answer. Adding phrases like 'Answer ONLY based on the following context. If the answer is not in the context, say I don't know.' constrains the model to use retrieved documents.
Option1: Add explicit prompt instructions to answer only from provided context
Option2: Remove the retrieved documents from the prompt
Option3: Increase the model temperature

QuestionText: Which TWO indicate that a knowledge base needs to be updated or expanded? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Frequent 'I don't know' responses to questions that should be answerable indicate missing documents in the knowledge base. Outdated answers that contradict current information indicate stale documents that need refreshing. Both signal that the knowledge base content does not match current user needs.
Option1: Frequent inability to answer questions that should be covered
Option2: Outdated answers contradicting current information
Option3: Fast response times

QuestionText: What is regression testing for AI applications and why is it critical?
Explanation: Regression testing reruns a suite of known test cases after any change (prompt updates, model switches, knowledge base updates) to verify that previously working functionality still works correctly. AI systems are particularly prone to regressions because small prompt changes can have unexpected cascading effects on response quality.
Option1: Rerunning test cases after changes to verify existing functionality still works
Option2: Testing the application for the first time
Option3: Load testing with increasing traffic

QuestionText: A company wants to compare two different foundation models for their use case. Which TWO evaluation approaches should they use? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Automated benchmarking on a representative evaluation dataset provides objective metrics (accuracy, latency, cost) across both models. Human evaluation with blind side-by-side comparisons captures subjective quality preferences that automated metrics miss. Using both approaches gives a complete picture for informed model selection.
Option1: Automated benchmarking on a representative evaluation dataset
Option2: Blind human side-by-side comparison
Option3: Selecting based on model name recognition
Option4: Choosing the model with more parameters

QuestionText: Which THREE are signs that an AI application needs prompt optimization? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Responses frequently exceeding the desired length waste tokens and increase cost. The model not following formatting instructions indicates unclear prompt structure. High token costs relative to response quality suggest the prompt includes unnecessary context or verbose instructions that could be trimmed without quality loss.
Option1: Responses frequently exceeding desired length
Option2: Model not following formatting instructions
Option3: High token costs relative to response quality

QuestionText: A deployed model occasionally generates responses in the wrong language. What is the most likely cause and fix?
Explanation: The system prompt likely does not explicitly specify the required response language. Multilingual models may switch languages based on input language or context. The fix is adding an explicit language instruction like 'Always respond in English regardless of the input language' to the system prompt.
Option1: Missing explicit language instruction in the system prompt
Option2: The model needs to be retrained
Option3: The VPC is configured for the wrong region
Option4: The S3 bucket has incorrect permissions

QuestionText: Which TWO are best practices for maintaining evaluation datasets over time? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Regularly adding new test cases from production edge cases and user-reported issues keeps the evaluation dataset representative of real-world usage. Versioning evaluation datasets alongside prompt versions enables tracking which test suite was used to validate each prompt change, supporting reproducibility and audit trails.
Option1: Add new test cases from production edge cases regularly
Option2: Version evaluation datasets alongside prompt versions
Option3: Use the same five test cases forever
Option4: Delete old test cases to save storage

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is building a RAG application using Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases. Which THREE components must they configure? (Select THREE)
Explanation: A data source (S3 bucket with documents) provides the content to index. An embedding model converts document chunks into vectors. A vector store (OpenSearch Serverless, Aurora, Pinecone) stores and searches the embeddings. Bedrock Knowledge Bases orchestrates the ingestion pipeline—chunking, embedding, and storing—and handles retrieval at query time.
Option1: A data source containing documents in S3
Option2: An embedding model for vector generation
Option3: A vector store for storing and searching embeddings
Option4: A relational database for storing raw documents
Option5: An EC2 instance for hosting the knowledge base

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to select a foundation model for a latency-sensitive chatbot application. Which TWO factors should they prioritize when comparing models on Amazon Bedrock? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Time to first token (TTFT) measures how quickly the model starts generating a response—critical for perceived responsiveness in chat. Output token throughput determines how fast the full response is delivered. Both directly impact user experience in real-time chat. Model size and training data volume are less directly relevant than actual inference performance metrics.
Option1: Time to first token (TTFT) for perceived responsiveness
Option2: Output token throughput for response delivery speed
Option3: Total number of model parameters
Option4: Size of the model's original training dataset

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is implementing a RAG pipeline and needs to choose a chunking strategy for their documents. The documents contain technical manuals with hierarchical sections. Which chunking approach is MOST appropriate?
Explanation: Hierarchical chunking preserves the document structure by creating chunks that respect section boundaries. Parent chunks contain broader context while child chunks contain specific details. During retrieval, the system can return the specific child chunk plus its parent context, providing both precision and sufficient context for the model to generate accurate answers.
Option1: Hierarchical chunking that respects document section boundaries with parent-child relationships
Option2: Fixed-size chunking with 100-token chunks and no overlap
Option3: Single chunk per document regardless of length

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application complies with GDPR data residency requirements. Which TWO configurations are essential? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Selecting a Bedrock Region within the EU (like eu-west-1 Ireland or eu-central-1 Frankfurt) ensures all data processing occurs within EU boundaries. Verifying that the chosen foundation models are available in that Region is essential since not all models are available in all Regions. Together these ensure data never leaves the required geographic boundary.
Option1: Deploy the application in an AWS Region within the required geographic boundary
Option2: Verify the required foundation models are available in the chosen Region
Option3: Use CloudFront to cache responses globally
Option4: Store data in US Regions and replicate to EU

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is building a generative AI application that processes customer PII. Which THREE data handling practices must they implement? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Encrypting data at rest (KMS) and in transit (TLS) protects PII from unauthorized access. Configuring Bedrock Guardrails with PII filters detects and redacts sensitive information in model inputs and outputs. Implementing data retention policies ensures PII is not stored longer than necessary. All three are essential for responsible PII handling in AI applications.
Option1: Encrypt data at rest with KMS and in transit with TLS
Option2: Configure Bedrock Guardrails with PII detection and redaction filters
Option3: Implement data retention policies to limit PII storage duration
Option4: Store PII in plaintext for easier processing
Option5: Share PII with model providers for better results

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their RAG application returns source citations with each generated answer. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases supports source attribution—when the model generates a response, it includes references to the specific document chunks that were used as context. The developer can configure the retrieval response to include the S3 URI, document title, and relevant text excerpt for each source. This enables users to verify the AI's answers against original documents.
Option1: Configure Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases to return source attribution with retrieved document references
Option2: Ask the model to make up citation URLs
Option3: Manually add citations after generation

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is evaluating embedding models for their semantic search application. Which metric is MOST important for evaluating embedding quality?
Explanation: Retrieval accuracy (measured by metrics like recall@k, MRR, or NDCG) directly measures how well the embeddings enable finding relevant documents for a given query. High-quality embeddings place semantically similar content close together in vector space, resulting in better retrieval. This is more meaningful than embedding dimension size or generation speed for search quality.
Option1: Retrieval accuracy metrics like recall@k and NDCG on representative queries
Option2: The dimensionality of the embedding vectors
Option3: The speed of embedding generation

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their foundation model has access to real-time data that changes frequently. RAG with static documents is insufficient. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Amazon Bedrock Agents with action groups can call APIs in real-time to fetch current data. When a user asks about real-time information (stock prices, order status, inventory levels), the agent reasons that it needs current data, calls the appropriate Lambda function (which queries the live data source), and incorporates the fresh results into its response.
Option1: Use Amazon Bedrock Agents with action groups that call APIs for real-time data retrieval
Option2: Fine-tune the model daily with updated data
Option3: Increase the RAG knowledge base refresh frequency to every second
Option4: Use a larger context window model

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is implementing a multi-turn conversation with a foundation model. How should they manage conversation history to stay within the context window limit?
