Option1: Log feature identifiers with each Bedrock call and aggregate token usage by feature for cost attribution
Option2: Track only the total monthly Bedrock bill
Option3: Estimate costs based on user count only

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that validates their generative AI application handles edge cases correctly. Which THREE edge cases should they test? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Empty or very short inputs test how the application handles minimal context. Very long inputs that exceed the context window test truncation and error handling. Inputs in unexpected languages test multilingual robustness. All three are common edge cases that can cause unexpected behavior if not handled properly in the application logic.
Option1: Empty or very short user inputs
Option2: Very long inputs that exceed the context window limit
Option3: Inputs in unexpected or unsupported languages
Option4: Inputs with correct grammar and spelling

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application handles multi-modal inputs—users can upload images and ask questions about them. Which Bedrock API parameter accepts image input?
Explanation: The Bedrock InvokeModel API accepts images as base64-encoded content within the messages array. For multimodal models like Claude 3, the content block includes both text and image objects. The image can be provided as base64 data or referenced from S3. The model processes both the image and text together to generate a response that addresses the visual content.
Option1: Include base64-encoded image data in the content block of the messages array alongside text
Option2: Upload images to a separate image processing API
Option3: Convert images to text descriptions before sending

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is building a RAG application for a legal firm. Documents contain highly specialized legal terminology. Which approach improves retrieval accuracy for domain-specific content?
Explanation: Fine-tuning the embedding model on domain-specific legal text improves its ability to capture legal semantic relationships. Legal terms that are semantically related (like 'indemnification' and 'hold harmless') will have more similar embeddings after domain-specific fine-tuning. Amazon Bedrock supports custom embedding models, and Amazon Titan Embeddings can be fine-tuned on domain data.
Option1: Fine-tune the embedding model on domain-specific legal text to improve semantic understanding of legal terminology
Option2: Use a general-purpose embedding model without any customization
Option3: Use keyword search instead of semantic search

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their application uses Amazon Bedrock to generate content that adheres to their company's brand voice and style guidelines. Which approach is MOST effective?
Explanation: A detailed system prompt that defines the brand voice (tone, vocabulary, sentence structure, personality) combined with few-shot examples of ideal responses is the most effective approach without fine-tuning. The system prompt acts as persistent instructions that shape every response. For even stronger brand adherence, fine-tuning on examples of brand-compliant content permanently adjusts the model's style.
Option1: Use a detailed system prompt defining brand voice with few-shot examples of ideal responses
Option2: Hope the model naturally matches the brand voice
Option3: Use a different model for each brand guideline

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their RAG application can handle queries that require information from multiple documents to answer completely. Which retrieval strategy should they use?
Explanation: Multi-query retrieval generates multiple search queries from the user's original question (using an LLM to rephrase from different angles), retrieves documents for each query, and merges the results. This captures information that a single query might miss. For example, 'Compare product A and product B' generates separate queries for each product, retrieving relevant information for both.
Option1: Use multi-query retrieval to generate multiple search queries and merge results for comprehensive coverage
Option2: Retrieve only the single most relevant document
Option3: Return all documents in the knowledge base

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application maintains compliance with data retention policies. Model invocation logs must be retained for 7 years then automatically deleted. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Bedrock model invocation logs stored in S3 can be managed with S3 Lifecycle policies that automatically delete objects after 7 years. For logs in CloudWatch, retention periods can be set on log groups. This ensures compliance with retention requirements without manual intervention. The developer should also ensure logs are encrypted and access-controlled throughout the retention period.
Option1: Configure S3 Lifecycle policies to delete invocation logs after 7 years and set CloudWatch Logs retention periods
Option2: Manually delete logs after 7 years
Option3: Store logs indefinitely without any retention policy

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is implementing a generative AI application that processes financial documents. Which TWO compliance requirements must they address? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Financial data must be encrypted at rest and in transit to meet regulatory requirements (SOX, PCI DSS, GLBA). Access to financial data and AI model outputs must be restricted to authorized personnel with audit logging to demonstrate compliance. Both are fundamental requirements for any application processing financial information.
Option1: Encrypt all financial data at rest and in transit
Option2: Implement strict access controls with comprehensive audit logging
Option3: Make financial data publicly accessible for transparency
Option4: Store financial data without encryption for faster processing

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application gracefully handles model errors and provides fallback responses. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Implementing a try-catch pattern around Bedrock API calls with fallback logic ensures the application remains functional even when the model is unavailable or returns errors. Fallback strategies include: trying an alternative model, returning a cached response, providing a generic helpful message, or escalating to a human agent. This prevents model errors from becoming user-facing failures.
Option1: Implement try-catch with fallback strategies including alternative models cached responses or human escalation
Option2: Let model errors propagate directly to the user
Option3: Retry the same request indefinitely

QuestionText: A developer is building a generative AI application that needs to maintain conversation context across multiple API calls. Which approach should they use with the Bedrock Converse API?
Explanation: The Bedrock Converse API accepts a messages array containing the full conversation history (alternating user and assistant messages). The developer maintains this array in their application state (or a database for persistence) and sends the complete history with each new request. The model uses the full conversation context to generate contextually appropriate responses.
Option1: Maintain a messages array with full conversation history and send it with each Converse API call
Option2: Rely on Bedrock to maintain conversation state between API calls
Option3: Send only the latest message without any history

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can generate and execute code safely. Which TWO safety measures should they implement? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Running generated code in a sandboxed environment (Lambda with minimal permissions, Docker container with no network access) prevents malicious or buggy code from affecting production systems. Code review by the model itself (asking the model to check for security issues) or static analysis tools catches common vulnerabilities before execution. Both are essential for safe code execution.
Option1: Execute generated code in a sandboxed environment with minimal permissions
Option2: Run static analysis or model-based security review on generated code before execution
Option3: Execute generated code directly in the production environment
Option4: Trust all generated code without any review

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application handles long-running tasks that exceed API timeout limits. Which architecture should they use?
Explanation: For long-running tasks (like processing large documents or generating extensive content), an asynchronous architecture using SQS and Lambda decouples the request from the response. The user submits a request, receives a task ID immediately, and the backend processes the task asynchronously. The user polls for results or receives a notification (via WebSocket or SNS) when complete.
Option1: Use an asynchronous architecture with SQS for task queuing and Lambda for processing with status polling or push notifications
Option2: Increase the API Gateway timeout to handle long tasks
Option3: Have the user wait for the synchronous response regardless of duration

QuestionText: A developer is building a generative AI application that needs to extract structured information from unstructured customer feedback emails. Which prompt engineering technique is MOST effective?
Explanation: Providing the exact output schema with field descriptions and a few-shot example of an email mapped to the structured output is the most effective approach. The model sees the expected format and learns the extraction pattern from examples. Adding instructions like 'Extract the following fields. If a field is not mentioned, use null' handles missing information gracefully.
Option1: Provide the output schema with field descriptions and few-shot examples of email-to-structured-data extraction
Option2: Ask the model to summarize the email in free text
Option3: Use regex patterns to extract fields

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application supports multiple output formats (JSON markdown plain text) based on the client's request. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: The application includes the desired output format in the system prompt or user prompt dynamically based on the client's format preference. For API clients requesting JSON, the prompt instructs 'Respond in valid JSON format.' For web clients, 'Respond in markdown format.' The same model and core prompt are used—only the format instruction changes per request.
Option1: Dynamically include the desired output format instruction in the prompt based on the client's preference
Option2: Deploy separate models for each output format
Option3: Always return JSON and let the client convert

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application can process a batch of requests efficiently for non-real-time use cases like nightly report generation. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Amazon Bedrock Batch Inference processes multiple requests asynchronously at a lower cost than real-time inference. The developer submits a batch of prompts as a JSONL file in S3, and Bedrock processes them in the background, writing results to S3. This is ideal for non-real-time use cases like report generation, content creation, and data processing where immediate responses are not needed.
Option1: Use Amazon Bedrock Batch Inference for cost-effective asynchronous processing of multiple requests
Option2: Send requests one at a time using the synchronous API
Option3: Use a single prompt with all requests concatenated

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application's Guardrails configuration is version-controlled and can be rolled back if issues are discovered. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Defining Guardrails configuration as infrastructure as code (CloudFormation or CDK) and storing it in a Git repository provides version control, code review, and rollback capabilities. Each Guardrails change goes through a pull request review process. If a configuration change causes issues, the developer can revert to the previous Git commit and redeploy the known-good configuration.
Option1: Define Guardrails configuration as infrastructure as code in Git with CloudFormation or CDK
Option2: Configure Guardrails manually in the console without version control
Option3: Document Guardrails settings in a spreadsheet

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application prevents users from using it to generate spam or bulk unsolicited content. Which TWO controls should they implement? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Rate limiting per user prevents any single user from generating excessive content that could be used for spam. Bedrock Guardrails with denied topics for spam-related content (bulk email generation, unsolicited messaging) blocks requests that explicitly aim to create spam. Together these provide both volume-based and content-based protection against spam generation.
Option1: Implement per-user rate limiting on API requests
Option2: Configure Bedrock Guardrails with denied topics for spam and bulk content generation
Option3: Allow unlimited content generation for all users

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application handles sensitive financial advice appropriately. The AI should provide general information but not specific investment recommendations. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Bedrock Guardrails denied topics can block specific investment recommendations while allowing general financial education. The system prompt explicitly instructs the model to provide general information only and direct users to qualified financial advisors for specific advice. A disclaimer is appended to relevant responses. This multi-layer approach ensures appropriate handling of financial content.
Option1: Configure denied topics for specific investment advice plus system prompt instructions to provide only general information with disclaimers
Option2: Allow the model to provide any financial advice without restrictions
Option3: Block all financial topics entirely

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application's safety controls are consistent across development staging and production environments. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Infrastructure as code (CloudFormation/CDK) defines Guardrails, IAM policies, and other safety configurations as code that is deployed identically across all environments. CI/CD pipelines ensure the same configuration is applied to dev, staging, and production. This prevents configuration drift where safety controls might be weaker in one environment than another.
Option1: Use infrastructure as code to deploy identical safety configurations across all environments via CI/CD
Option2: Configure safety controls manually in each environment
Option3: Only implement safety controls in production

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to optimize the performance of their Bedrock Knowledge Base retrieval. Queries are returning too many irrelevant results. Which TWO optimizations should they implement? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Adjusting the similarity score threshold filters out results below a minimum relevance score, ensuring only sufficiently relevant documents are returned. Reducing the number of retrieved results (top-k) from a high value to a more focused number (3-5) eliminates marginally relevant results that dilute the context. Both improve the signal-to-noise ratio of retrieved content.
Option1: Increase the similarity score threshold to filter out low-relevance results
Option2: Reduce the top-k parameter to return fewer more relevant results
Option3: Increase top-k to return more results

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that minimizes cold start latency for their Lambda-based Bedrock application. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Lambda Provisioned Concurrency keeps a specified number of function instances initialized and ready to respond immediately, eliminating cold start latency. For a Bedrock application where cold starts add noticeable delay (SDK initialization, connection setup), provisioned concurrency ensures consistent low latency. The developer configures enough provisioned instances to handle expected baseline traffic.
Option1: Configure Lambda Provisioned Concurrency to eliminate cold start latency
Option2: Accept cold start latency as unavoidable
Option3: Use larger Lambda memory to reduce cold starts

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that tests their Bedrock Guardrails configuration to ensure it blocks harmful content while allowing legitimate requests. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Creating a test suite with both positive cases (legitimate requests that should pass) and negative cases (harmful requests that should be blocked) validates that Guardrails is correctly configured. Running this test suite after every Guardrails configuration change ensures that new rules don't accidentally block legitimate content or allow harmful content through.
Option1: Create a test suite with positive and negative test cases and run it after every Guardrails configuration change
Option2: Test with a single example and assume it works for all cases
Option3: Deploy Guardrails without testing

QuestionText: A developer's generative AI application is producing responses that are factually correct but not in the expected format. The model sometimes returns markdown when plain text is expected. Which TWO fixes should they apply? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Adding explicit format instructions in the system prompt ('Always respond in plain text without any markdown formatting') directly addresses the issue. Adding output post-processing that strips markdown formatting provides a safety net for cases where the model doesn't follow instructions perfectly. Both approaches work together—prompt instructions reduce the problem, post-processing eliminates remaining cases.
Option1: Add explicit format instructions in the system prompt
Option2: Implement output post-processing to strip unwanted formatting
Option3: Switch to a different foundation model

QuestionText: A developer needs to troubleshoot why their Bedrock Knowledge Base is not returning relevant results for queries. Which THREE areas should they investigate? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Chunk size and overlap settings affect whether relevant information is captured in retrievable units. The embedding model's suitability for the domain determines whether semantic relationships are captured correctly. The vector store's similarity metric (cosine vs. dot product vs. Euclidean) affects how similarity is calculated. All three directly impact retrieval quality.
Option1: Check chunk size and overlap settings for appropriate document segmentation
Option2: Verify the embedding model captures domain-specific semantic relationships
Option3: Review the vector store similarity metric configuration
Option4: Check the foundation model's temperature setting
Option5: Review the Lambda function's memory allocation

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can be quickly switched between different foundation models without code changes. Which design pattern should they use?
Explanation: An abstraction layer (adapter pattern) wraps the Bedrock API calls behind a common interface. Model-specific details (prompt formatting, parameter names, response parsing) are encapsulated in model-specific adapters. Switching models requires only changing a configuration value (model ID), not application code. This enables A/B testing, model upgrades, and fallback strategies without code deployments.
Option1: Implement an abstraction layer with model-specific adapters that can be switched via configuration
Option2: Hardcode model-specific API calls throughout the application
Option3: Deploy separate applications for each model

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their RAG application provides answers with confidence indicators so users know how reliable each answer is. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: The retrieval similarity score indicates how well the retrieved documents match the query. High scores suggest the knowledge base contains relevant information, increasing answer confidence. Low scores suggest the answer may be less reliable. The developer can map score ranges to confidence levels (high/medium/low) displayed to users, helping them calibrate trust in each response.
Option1: Use retrieval similarity scores mapped to confidence levels displayed to users
Option2: Always display high confidence regardless of retrieval quality
Option3: Never show confidence indicators to avoid confusing users

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application handles personally identifiable information (PII) in compliance with CCPA. Which THREE requirements must they address? (Select THREE)
Explanation: CCPA requires that consumers can request disclosure of what personal information is collected (right to know). Consumers can request deletion of their personal information (right to delete). The application must not discriminate against consumers who exercise their CCPA rights. The developer must implement mechanisms to fulfill these rights for any PII processed by the AI application.
Option1: Implement the right to know—disclose what PII is collected and processed
Option2: Implement the right to delete—enable deletion of consumer PII on request
Option3: Ensure non-discrimination against consumers who exercise their CCPA rights
Option4: Collect as much PII as possible for better AI performance

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application can dynamically adjust the model's behavior based on the conversation context. For example using a more creative style for brainstorming and a more precise style for factual questions. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: The application analyzes the user's intent (brainstorming vs. factual question) and dynamically adjusts inference parameters. For creative tasks, higher temperature and top-p values produce more diverse outputs. For factual tasks, lower temperature produces more precise, deterministic outputs. The system prompt can also be dynamically adjusted to include style-specific instructions based on the detected intent.
Option1: Dynamically adjust temperature top-p and system prompt instructions based on detected user intent
Option2: Use the same parameters for all types of requests
Option3: Create separate applications for creative and factual tasks

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can handle file uploads (PDFs Word documents) and answer questions about the uploaded content. Which architecture should they use?
Explanation: The application stores uploaded files in S3, extracts text using Textract (for scanned documents) or direct parsing (for digital PDFs/Word), chunks the content, generates embeddings, and stores them in a session-specific vector index. The user's questions are answered using RAG against this session-specific index. For persistent document access, the content is added to the main knowledge base.
Option1: Extract text from uploads store in S3 create session-specific embeddings and use RAG to answer questions about the content
Option2: Send the entire file content in every prompt
Option3: Ask users to copy-paste document text manually

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that tracks the total cost of their generative AI application broken down by feature. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Tagging Bedrock API calls with feature identifiers (via custom headers or application-level logging) and correlating with CloudWatch metrics enables per-feature cost tracking. The application logs the feature name, model ID, input tokens, and output tokens for each request. A dashboard aggregates costs by feature, enabling data-driven decisions about which features justify their AI costs.
Option1: Log feature identifiers with each Bedrock call and aggregate token usage by feature for cost attribution
Option2: Track only the total monthly Bedrock bill
Option3: Estimate costs based on user count only

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that validates their generative AI application handles edge cases correctly. Which THREE edge cases should they test? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Empty or very short inputs test how the application handles minimal context. Very long inputs that exceed the context window test truncation and error handling. Inputs in unexpected languages test multilingual robustness. All three are common edge cases that can cause unexpected behavior if not handled properly in the application logic.
Option1: Empty or very short user inputs
Option2: Very long inputs that exceed the context window limit
Option3: Inputs in unexpected or unsupported languages
Option4: Inputs with correct grammar and spelling

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their RAG application handles queries that span multiple topics requiring information from different document collections. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases supports multiple data sources within a single knowledge base. The developer can configure separate S3 prefixes or buckets for different document collections (HR policies, technical docs, product info). During retrieval, the system searches across all data sources and returns the most relevant results regardless of which collection they come from.
Option1: Configure multiple data sources within a single Bedrock Knowledge Base for cross-collection retrieval
Option2: Create separate applications for each document collection
Option3: Merge all documents into a single file

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is implementing a generative AI application and needs to handle the model's tendency to be overly verbose. Which TWO techniques reduce response length? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Setting a lower max_tokens value directly limits the response length at the API level. Including explicit length instructions in the prompt ('Respond in 2-3 sentences' or 'Be concise') guides the model to generate shorter responses. Both work together—the prompt instruction shapes the model's intent while max_tokens provides a hard limit.
Option1: Set a lower max_tokens value to limit response length
Option2: Include explicit conciseness instructions in the prompt
Option3: Increase the temperature for shorter responses
Option4: Use a larger model for more concise output

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can be audited to show that no training data was used from specific restricted sources. Which Bedrock feature helps demonstrate this?
Explanation: Amazon Bedrock does not use customer inputs or outputs to train base foundation models—this is a contractual guarantee documented in the AWS service terms. The developer can reference AWS's data privacy documentation and Bedrock's service terms to demonstrate to auditors that their data is not used for model training. Model invocation logging provides evidence of what data was processed.
Option1: Reference Bedrock's contractual guarantee that customer data is not used for model training plus invocation logging for audit evidence
Option2: Request the model's complete training dataset from AWS
Option3: Assume all data is used for training

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their RAG application supports hybrid search combining both semantic and keyword matching. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Amazon OpenSearch Serverless (used as a vector store for Bedrock Knowledge Bases) supports hybrid search that combines semantic vector search with traditional keyword (BM25) search. Hybrid search returns results that are both semantically relevant AND contain specific keywords, improving retrieval for queries that include technical terms, product names, or codes that semantic search alone might miss.
Option1: Use Amazon OpenSearch Serverless with hybrid search combining semantic and keyword matching
Option2: Use only semantic search for all queries
Option3: Use only keyword search for all queries

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application processes customer data in compliance with HIPAA. Which THREE requirements must they meet? (Select THREE)
Explanation: A Business Associate Agreement (BAA) with AWS is required before processing PHI on any AWS service. Encryption of PHI at rest and in transit is a HIPAA requirement. Access controls ensuring only authorized personnel can access PHI, with audit logging of all access, are fundamental HIPAA safeguards. All three are mandatory for HIPAA-compliant AI applications.
Option1: Execute a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) with AWS
Option2: Encrypt all PHI at rest and in transit
Option3: Implement access controls and audit logging for all PHI access
Option4: Store PHI in publicly accessible S3 buckets

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer is building a RAG application and needs to ensure that the knowledge base index stays performant as the document collection grows to millions of documents. Which TWO strategies help maintain performance at scale? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Metadata filtering reduces the search space by applying filters before vector similarity search, keeping query latency constant regardless of total collection size. Partitioning the vector index by logical boundaries (department, document type, date range) enables targeted searches within relevant partitions. Both prevent query latency from degrading as the collection grows.
Option1: Use metadata filtering to reduce the search space before vector similarity search
Option2: Partition the vector index by logical boundaries for targeted searches
Option3: Search the entire index for every query
Option4: Reduce the number of documents to maintain performance

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can generate and send emails on behalf of users after getting their approval. Which architecture ensures human oversight?
Explanation: The application generates the email draft using Bedrock, presents it to the user for review and approval, and only sends it after explicit user confirmation. This human-in-the-loop pattern ensures the AI never sends emails autonomously. The approval step can be implemented as a UI confirmation dialog or an approval workflow using Step Functions with a human task.
Option1: Generate email drafts with Bedrock present to user for review and send only after explicit approval
Option2: Automatically send AI-generated emails without user review
Option3: Send emails first and let users recall them if needed

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application can handle requests that require multiple sequential model calls with different prompts. Which pattern should they use?
Explanation: Prompt chaining passes the output of one model call as input to the next. For example: Call 1 extracts key entities from text, Call 2 classifies the entities, Call 3 generates a summary using the classified entities. Each step uses a focused prompt for a specific subtask, producing better results than a single complex prompt. Step Functions or application code orchestrates the chain.
Option1: Implement prompt chaining where each model call's output feeds into the next call's input
Option2: Combine all tasks into a single massive prompt
Option3: Run all calls in parallel with no dependencies

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application provides real-time suggestions as users type similar to autocomplete. Which architecture minimizes latency?
Explanation: Streaming inference with a small fast model provides the lowest latency for autocomplete. The application sends partial input to Bedrock as the user types (debounced to avoid excessive calls), uses a small model for fast inference, and streams the suggestion back token by token. The small model's lower latency is more important than a large model's quality for autocomplete use cases.
Option1: Use streaming inference with a small fast model and debounced input for real-time autocomplete
Option2: Use the largest available model for best quality suggestions
Option3: Generate suggestions in batch every 5 seconds

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application can process structured data from a database and generate natural language insights. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: The application queries the database for relevant data, formats it as a structured context (table, JSON, or descriptive text) in the prompt, and instructs the model to analyze the data and generate insights. The prompt includes specific analysis instructions ('Identify trends, anomalies, and key metrics'). The model's language understanding transforms raw data into actionable natural language insights.
Option1: Query the database format results as structured context in the prompt and instruct the model to generate insights
Option2: Fine-tune the model on the database contents
Option3: Store all database data in the model's context permanently

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application supports undo/regenerate functionality allowing users to request a different response. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Maintaining the conversation history in application state allows the developer to remove the last assistant message and re-send the conversation to the model with the same (or modified) user message. Using a different random seed or slightly higher temperature produces a different response. The application stores multiple response versions so users can compare and select their preferred answer.
Option1: Remove the last response from conversation history and re-invoke the model with modified parameters for a different response
Option2: Tell the user that responses cannot be regenerated
Option3: Cache only one response per conversation

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application maintains a complete audit trail that can withstand legal scrutiny. Which THREE elements must the audit trail include? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Timestamps with millisecond precision establish exactly when each interaction occurred. The complete input and output content documents what was asked and what the AI generated. The identity of the user (authenticated via Cognito or IAM) establishes who initiated each interaction. Together these provide the who, what, and when required for legally defensible audit trails.
Option1: Timestamps with millisecond precision for each interaction
Option2: Complete input prompts and model outputs
Option3: Authenticated user identity for each request

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application prevents data leakage between different customer tenants in a multi-tenant architecture. Which TWO controls should they implement? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Tenant-specific IAM roles ensure each tenant's requests can only access their own data (S3 prefixes, DynamoDB items, knowledge base partitions). Tenant isolation in the knowledge base (separate data sources or metadata-filtered retrieval per tenant) prevents one tenant's queries from retrieving another tenant's documents. Both are essential for multi-tenant data isolation.
Option1: Use tenant-specific IAM roles that restrict data access to each tenant's resources
Option2: Implement tenant isolation in the knowledge base with metadata filtering or separate data sources
Option3: Use a single shared IAM role for all tenants
Option4: Store all tenant data in the same unpartitioned knowledge base

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application's model access can be revoked immediately if a security incident is detected. Which approach enables this?
Explanation: IAM policies attached to the application's role can be modified immediately to deny bedrock:InvokeModel, cutting off model access within seconds. For more granular control, AWS Organizations SCPs can deny Bedrock access across entire accounts. The developer should have a pre-built 'emergency deny' policy ready to attach, and automate the attachment via a Lambda function triggered by security alerts.
Option1: Maintain a pre-built IAM deny policy that can be immediately attached to revoke Bedrock access
Option2: Manually contact AWS support to disable the model
Option3: Wait for the model provider to revoke access

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that optimizes their Bedrock application's cost by routing simple queries to a cheaper model and complex queries to a more capable model. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: A query classifier (which can itself be a small, cheap model or rule-based logic) analyzes each incoming query and routes it to the appropriate model. Simple queries (greetings, FAQs, yes/no questions) go to a small, fast, cheap model. Complex queries (multi-step reasoning, analysis, creative writing) go to a larger, more capable model. This optimizes cost without sacrificing quality where it matters.
Option1: Implement a query classifier that routes simple queries to a cheap model and complex queries to a capable model
Option2: Use the most expensive model for all queries to ensure quality
Option3: Use the cheapest model for all queries to minimize cost

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that reduces the latency of their RAG application's end-to-end response time. Which THREE optimizations should they apply? (Select THREE)
Explanation: Parallel retrieval and model invocation (start generating with initial context while retrieving more) reduces total time. Streaming responses show tokens immediately rather than waiting for completion. Pre-computing embeddings for common queries eliminates embedding generation latency for frequent questions. All three reduce different components of the end-to-end latency.
Option1: Parallelize retrieval and model invocation where possible
Option2: Stream responses to show tokens immediately
Option3: Pre-compute and cache embeddings for common queries
Option4: Use synchronous blocking calls for all operations

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that monitors their generative AI application's quality in production without manual review of every response. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Automated quality monitoring samples a percentage of production responses and evaluates them using an LLM-as-judge (a separate model that scores responses on relevance, accuracy, and helpfulness). Scores are tracked in CloudWatch over time. Degradation triggers alerts for human review. This provides continuous quality monitoring at scale without reviewing every response manually.
Option1: Sample production responses and evaluate with LLM-as-judge scoring tracked in CloudWatch with degradation alerts
Option2: Manually review every response
Option3: Assume quality is constant and never monitor

QuestionText: A developer's RAG application is returning answers that contradict information in the retrieved documents. The retrieval is correct but the model ignores the context. Which TWO fixes should they apply? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Strengthening the system prompt with explicit instructions like 'Base your answer ONLY on the provided context. Do not use prior knowledge.' forces the model to prioritize retrieved context. Lowering the temperature reduces the model's tendency to generate creative (but potentially contradictory) content, making it more likely to stick closely to the provided context.
Option1: Strengthen the system prompt to explicitly instruct the model to base answers only on provided context
Option2: Lower the temperature to reduce creative deviation from the context
Option3: Increase the temperature for more diverse answers
Option4: Remove the retrieved context from the prompt

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement load testing for their Bedrock application to ensure it handles production traffic. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Load testing with realistic traffic patterns (varying query complexity, concurrent users, peak/off-peak patterns) reveals how the application behaves under production-like conditions. The developer should monitor Bedrock throttling rates, Lambda concurrency, response latency percentiles (p50, p95, p99), and error rates during the test. Results inform capacity planning and identify bottlenecks.
Option1: Conduct load testing with realistic traffic patterns monitoring throttling latency percentiles and error rates
Option2: Test with a single request and extrapolate
Option3: Skip load testing and hope production traffic is manageable

QuestionText: A developer's Bedrock application is returning 'AccessDeniedException' when calling InvokeModel. Which THREE things should they check? (Select THREE)
Explanation: The IAM role must have bedrock:InvokeModel permission for the specific model ARN. The model must be enabled (requested access) in the Bedrock console for the account. The request must be made in a Region where the model is available. All three are common causes of AccessDeniedException that the developer should verify in order.
Option1: Verify the IAM role has bedrock:InvokeModel permission for the specific model ARN
Option2: Verify the model has been enabled in the Bedrock console for the account
Option3: Verify the request is being made in a Region where the model is available
Option4: Check if the S3 bucket policy allows Bedrock access
Option5: Verify the VPC security group allows outbound traffic

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can switch between models without changing the prompt format. Which Bedrock API should they use?
Explanation: The Bedrock Converse API provides a unified interface across all supported models. Unlike the InvokeModel API (which requires model-specific request/response formats), Converse uses a standardized messages format that works with any model. Switching models requires only changing the modelId parameter—the prompt format remains identical. This simplifies multi-model applications and A/B testing.
Option1: Use the Bedrock Converse API which provides a unified message format across all models
Option2: Use the InvokeModel API with model-specific formatting for each model
Option3: Create separate API integrations for each model

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their RAG application handles documents in PDF format that contain scanned images of text. Which preprocessing step is required before indexing?
Explanation: Scanned PDF pages are images, not searchable text. Amazon Textract performs OCR (Optical Character Recognition) to extract text from scanned document images. The extracted text is then chunked, embedded, and indexed in the knowledge base. Without this preprocessing step, the RAG system cannot search or retrieve information from scanned documents.
Option1: Use Amazon Textract to extract text from scanned PDF images before indexing in the knowledge base
Option2: Index the PDF files directly without text extraction
Option3: Ask users to retype scanned documents

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can generate responses that include formatted tables and lists. Which approach ensures consistent formatting?
Explanation: Including explicit formatting instructions and examples in the prompt ('Format the comparison as a markdown table with columns: Feature, Product A, Product B') guides the model to produce consistently formatted output. Providing a few-shot example of the desired table format further reinforces the expected structure. The application's frontend then renders the markdown appropriately.
Option1: Include explicit formatting instructions with examples in the prompt and render markdown in the frontend
Option2: Hope the model formats output correctly without instructions
Option3: Post-process all output to remove formatting

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application handles conversation branching—users can go back to a previous point in the conversation and explore a different direction. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Storing the complete conversation tree (not just a linear history) in the application state enables branching. Each branch point stores the conversation state at that moment. When a user wants to explore a different direction, the application loads the state from the branch point and continues from there. This requires a tree data structure rather than a simple message array.
Option1: Store conversation state as a tree structure enabling users to branch from any previous point
Option2: Only maintain a single linear conversation history
Option3: Start a new conversation for each direction

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application's responses include appropriate disclaimers for regulated content areas like financial or medical information. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Configuring the system prompt to include domain-specific disclaimer instructions ('When discussing financial topics, always include: This is general information and not financial advice. Consult a qualified financial advisor.') ensures disclaimers are consistently included. Bedrock Guardrails can be configured to detect regulated content topics and the application can append standard disclaimers programmatically.
Option1: Configure system prompt disclaimer instructions for regulated topics and programmatically append standard disclaimers when regulated content is detected
Option2: Never include disclaimers to keep responses concise
Option3: Add disclaimers to every response regardless of content

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that reduces their Bedrock application's cost during off-peak hours when traffic drops significantly. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: For applications using Provisioned Throughput, the developer can use scheduled scaling—reducing provisioned capacity during off-peak hours and increasing it before peak hours. For on-demand pricing, costs naturally decrease with lower traffic. The developer can also implement more aggressive caching during off-peak hours when response freshness is less critical, further reducing model invocations.
Option1: Use scheduled scaling of Provisioned Throughput capacity aligned with traffic patterns
Option2: Maintain peak capacity 24/7 regardless of traffic
Option3: Shut down the application during off-peak hours

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that validates their generative AI application produces consistent results for deterministic queries. Which testing approach should they use?
Explanation: Setting temperature to 0 (or near 0) makes the model deterministic—the same input produces the same output. The developer creates a test suite of deterministic queries with expected outputs and runs them with temperature 0. Any deviation from expected output indicates a problem (prompt change, model update, configuration drift). This regression testing catches unintended changes.
Option1: Run deterministic test queries with temperature 0 and compare outputs against expected results
Option2: Test with high temperature and accept any output
Option3: Test only once and assume consistency

QuestionText: A developer's Bedrock application is experiencing intermittent 'ServiceUnavailableException' errors. Which TWO approaches should they implement? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Implementing retry logic with exponential backoff handles transient service unavailability by waiting progressively longer between retries, giving the service time to recover. Configuring a fallback model provides an alternative when the primary model is unavailable—if Model A returns ServiceUnavailable, the application automatically tries Model B. Both improve application resilience.
Option1: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient errors
Option2: Configure a fallback model that is used when the primary model is unavailable
Option3: Fail immediately without retrying
Option4: Switch to a self-hosted model permanently

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can reason about time-sensitive information. The model should know the current date and not provide outdated information. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Including the current date and time in the system prompt ('Today's date is March 31, 2026') gives the model temporal awareness. Combined with RAG that retrieves recent documents, the model can reason about time-sensitive information accurately. Without this, the model has no concept of 'now' and may provide outdated information based on its training data cutoff.
Option1: Include the current date in the system prompt and use RAG with recent documents for time-sensitive queries
Option2: Rely on the model's training data for current information
Option3: Fine-tune the model daily with current events

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their RAG application can answer questions that require synthesizing information from multiple retrieved documents. Which prompt engineering technique helps with this?
Explanation: Instructing the model to 'Synthesize information from all provided sources to give a comprehensive answer' in the system prompt encourages the model to combine information across multiple retrieved chunks rather than answering from a single chunk. Providing the retrieved chunks with clear source labels helps the model attribute and cross-reference information from different documents.
Option1: Instruct the model to synthesize across all provided sources with clear source labeling for cross-referencing
Option2: Retrieve only one document per query
Option3: Ask the model to pick the single best source

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application handles ambiguous user queries by asking clarifying questions before generating a response. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: The system prompt instructs the model: 'If the user's question is ambiguous or could be interpreted multiple ways, ask a clarifying question before answering.' This teaches the model to identify ambiguity and seek clarification rather than guessing. The conversation continues naturally once the user provides clarification, leading to more accurate and relevant responses.
Option1: Include instructions in the system prompt to ask clarifying questions when queries are ambiguous
Option2: Always guess the user's intent without asking
Option3: Return an error for ambiguous queries

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application can generate responses that reference specific sections of retrieved documents with page numbers. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Including page numbers and section identifiers in the document metadata during RAG indexing enables the model to reference specific locations. When chunks are retrieved, their metadata (page number, section title) is included in the context. The prompt instructs the model to cite page numbers when referencing information. This provides verifiable citations users can look up.
Option1: Include page numbers and section identifiers in document metadata during indexing and instruct the model to cite them
Option2: Ask the model to guess page numbers
Option3: Remove all metadata from retrieved chunks

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can handle multi-language conversations where the user switches languages mid-conversation. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Multilingual foundation models (like Claude 3) naturally handle language switching within conversations. The system prompt instruction 'Always respond in the same language as the user's most recent message' ensures the model matches the user's language. The model detects the language of each message and responds accordingly, even when the user switches languages mid-conversation.
Option1: Use a multilingual model with system prompt instructions to match the user's language in each response
Option2: Require users to select a language at the start and never switch
Option3: Translate all messages to English before processing

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application provides progressive responses—first a quick summary then detailed analysis if the user wants more. Which pattern should they use?
Explanation: The application first calls the model with a prompt requesting a brief summary (low max_tokens, concise instructions). If the user requests more detail, a follow-up call with the original context plus 'Provide a detailed analysis' generates the comprehensive response. This progressive disclosure pattern provides fast initial responses while offering depth on demand.
Option1: Generate a brief summary first then provide detailed analysis on user request using follow-up model calls
Option2: Always generate the longest possible response
Option3: Generate only summaries with no option for detail

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application logs are tamper-proof for regulatory compliance. Which TWO AWS features provide this? (Select TWO)
Explanation: CloudTrail log file integrity validation creates digitally signed digest files that prove logs have not been modified. S3 Object Lock (compliance mode) prevents anyone—including root users—from deleting or modifying log files during the retention period. Together these provide cryptographic proof of log integrity and physical prevention of tampering.
Option1: Enable CloudTrail log file integrity validation for cryptographic tamper detection
Option2: Store logs in S3 with Object Lock compliance mode to prevent modification
Option3: Store logs on an EC2 instance's local disk
Option4: Use standard S3 storage without Object Lock

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can be completely shut down within minutes if a critical safety issue is discovered. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: A pre-built 'kill switch' using an IAM deny policy that blocks all Bedrock API calls can be attached to the application's role within seconds. Automating this via a Lambda function triggered by a CloudWatch Alarm or manual button in a dashboard enables rapid shutdown. The developer should test this kill switch regularly to ensure it works when needed.
Option1: Maintain a pre-built IAM deny policy and automated Lambda trigger for rapid Bedrock access revocation
Option2: Manually delete the application's IAM role
Option3: Contact AWS support to disable the account

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application prevents the model from generating content that could be used for academic dishonesty. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Bedrock Guardrails denied topics can be configured to block requests for essay writing, homework completion, exam answers, and other academic dishonesty patterns. The system prompt can instruct the model to help users learn and understand concepts rather than providing complete answers. Together these guide the model toward educational assistance rather than answer generation.
Option1: Configure Guardrails denied topics for academic dishonesty patterns and system prompt instructions for educational guidance
Option2: Allow all academic content generation without restrictions
Option3: Block all educational content entirely

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that provides cost forecasting for their Bedrock application based on projected user growth. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Tracking current token usage per user (average input/output tokens per session) and multiplying by projected user growth provides a cost forecast. The developer logs token usage metrics to CloudWatch, calculates per-user averages, and projects costs based on growth estimates. AWS Cost Explorer's forecasting feature can also project Bedrock costs based on historical trends.
Option1: Track per-user token usage metrics and project costs based on user growth estimates combined with Cost Explorer forecasting
Option2: Assume costs will remain constant regardless of user growth
Option3: Wait until costs become a problem before forecasting

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that tests their generative AI application's behavior when the Bedrock service is degraded or unavailable. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Chaos engineering principles applied to AI applications involve simulating Bedrock failures (using fault injection or mock responses) to verify the application handles degradation gracefully. The developer tests: fallback model switching, cached response serving, graceful error messages, and circuit breaker activation. This ensures the application remains functional during service disruptions.
Option1: Simulate Bedrock failures to test fallback mechanisms cached responses and error handling
Option2: Assume Bedrock will always be available
Option3: Test only during actual Bedrock outages

QuestionText: A developer's generative AI application is producing responses that are technically correct but not helpful to users. Users report the answers are too generic. Which TWO improvements should they make? (Select TWO)
Explanation: Adding more specific context through RAG (retrieving more targeted documents) gives the model better information to generate specific, helpful answers. Improving the system prompt with more specific instructions about the desired level of detail, examples of good responses, and the target audience helps the model calibrate its responses to user expectations.
Option1: Improve RAG retrieval to provide more specific and targeted context
Option2: Enhance the system prompt with specific instructions about desired detail level and audience
Option3: Increase the model's temperature for more creative responses

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can handle concurrent users with different permission levels accessing the same knowledge base. Which approach ensures proper access control?
Explanation: Lake Formation or metadata-based filtering in the knowledge base restricts which documents each user can access based on their permissions. The application authenticates the user (Cognito), determines their permission level, and includes the appropriate metadata filter in the retrieval query. Users only see answers derived from documents they are authorized to access.
Option1: Apply user-permission-based metadata filters to knowledge base retrieval queries based on authenticated user roles
Option2: Give all users access to all documents
Option3: Create separate knowledge bases for each permission level

Category: Foundation Model Integration Data Management and Compliance
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their RAG application's knowledge base supports incremental updates without re-indexing the entire document collection. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases supports incremental sync—when StartIngestionJob is called, it detects new, modified, and deleted files in the S3 data source and updates only the affected embeddings. Unchanged documents are not re-processed. This makes updates efficient even for large knowledge bases, as only the delta is processed.
Option1: Use Bedrock Knowledge Base incremental sync which processes only new modified and deleted documents
Option2: Re-index the entire knowledge base for every update
Option3: Delete and recreate the knowledge base for each update

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their generative AI application can generate responses that include clickable links to source documents. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: When Bedrock Knowledge Bases returns retrieved chunks, each chunk includes metadata with the S3 URI of the source document. The application maps these S3 URIs to user-accessible URLs (pre-signed S3 URLs or corresponding web URLs). The model's response includes references that the application renders as clickable links, enabling users to verify information against original sources.
Option1: Map retrieved chunk S3 URIs to user-accessible URLs and render them as clickable links in the response
Option2: Ask the model to generate URLs from memory
Option3: Remove all source references from responses

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution where their Bedrock application can handle requests that require mathematical calculations. Foundation models are unreliable at math. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Bedrock Agents with a calculator action group (Lambda function that performs mathematical operations) offloads calculations to deterministic code. The agent recognizes when a calculation is needed, calls the calculator action group with the numbers and operation, and incorporates the exact result into its response. This combines the model's language understanding with reliable computation.
Option1: Use a Bedrock Agent with a calculator action group that performs calculations in Lambda
Option2: Trust the foundation model to perform calculations accurately
Option3: Ask users to calculate manually

Category: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that optimizes their Bedrock application for both cost and quality. Some responses require high quality while others are acceptable at lower quality. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: Implementing a quality tier system routes requests to different models based on quality requirements. Critical customer-facing responses use a large, high-quality model. Internal or low-stakes responses use a smaller, cheaper model. The routing logic can be rule-based (by feature/endpoint) or dynamic (based on query complexity analysis). This optimizes the cost-quality tradeoff across the application.
Option1: Implement a quality tier system that routes requests to different models based on quality requirements
Option2: Use the highest quality model for everything
Option3: Use the lowest cost model for everything

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that catches regressions in their generative AI application when the underlying foundation model is updated by the provider. Which approach should they use?
Explanation: A regression test suite with pinned expected outputs (or acceptable output ranges evaluated by LLM-as-judge) runs automatically when model versions change. The developer maintains a golden dataset of inputs with expected outputs. After a model update, the test suite runs and compares new outputs against expectations. Significant deviations trigger alerts for human review before the update is accepted.
Option1: Maintain a regression test suite that runs automatically when model versions change with deviation alerting
Option2: Accept all model updates without testing
Option3: Pin to a specific model version and never update

QuestionText: A developer needs to implement a solution that helps them understand why their RAG application retrieved specific documents for a query. Which debugging approach should they use?
Explanation: Logging the retrieval query embedding, the similarity scores for all candidate documents, and the final ranked results provides full visibility into the retrieval process. The developer can see which documents scored highest, why certain documents were or weren't retrieved, and whether the similarity threshold is appropriate. This debugging information is essential for tuning retrieval quality.
Option1: Log retrieval embeddings similarity scores and ranked results to understand and debug the retrieval process
Option2: Only log the final model response
Option3: Disable logging to improve performance
